title: Palicourea longicuspis
intro: Palicourea longicuspis is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "long-cusped palicourea" due to its distinctive elongated leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea longicuspis include alkaloids, particularly palicoureen and longicuspine, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in the production of medicinal extracts and tinctures.

The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest families of flowering plants and includes a wide variety of species, ranging from coffee plants to gardenias.
